name: mql-trading-dev-validation
description: Comprehensive quality gates and validation standards for MQL4/MQL5 and C/C++ MetaTrader development

project_detection.description: Verify this is an MQL4/MQL5 (MetaTrader) project before running validation
project_detection.skip_message: Skipping MQL validation: No MQL4/MQL5 (MetaTrader) project detected in workspace

phase_1_compilation.description: MetaEditor command-line compilation and build verification

checks.name: metaeditor_compile
checks.description: Compile .mq5/.mq4 via MetaEditor command line; inspect the generated .log next to the source for errors and warnings
checks.note: MetaEditor is a Windows GUI-dependent tool with no headless CI equivalent. The /compile flag works only where MetaEditor (metaeditor64.exe for MT5, metaeditor.exe for MT4) is installed and on PATH. Skip this check if MetaEditor is unavailable in the environment and fall back to the manual compilation review checklist.

checks.name: compile_log_zero_errors
checks.description: Parse the compilation .log and confirm zero errors before proceeding
checks.note: MQL compile output is written to a sibling .log file, not stdout. A non-existent or stale .log means the compile did not run.

checks.name: manual_compilation_review
checks.description: Fallback when MetaEditor is unavailable: review code for syntactically valid MQL
checklist: No use of MQL5-only constructs in .mq4 files and no deprecated MQL4 patterns in .mq5 files
All #include and #import paths resolve
MQL4 files declare #property strict; MQL5 files do not (strict is MQL4-only)
Embedded #resource references point at files that exist

phase_2_mql_correctness.description: MQL4/MQL5 correctness review (timeseries, data access, indicator architecture)

checks.name: data_access_return_checks
checks.description: Every data-copy call validates its return value
checklist: Every CopyBuffer / CopyRates / CopyTime / CopyHigh / CopyLow / CopyClose / CopyTickVolume return value is checked against the requested count
GetLastError() is read immediately after a failing copy call (it resets on the next API call), with ERR_HISTORY_WILL_UPDATED handled as a retry rather than a hard error
Invalid or missing data returns EMPTY_VALUE, never 0.0 (0.0 is a valid Fisher/indicator value)

checks.name: timeseries_orientation
checklist: ArraySetAsSeries(array, true) is called on every timeseries array before use in MQL5
Arrays are declared dynamic (double arr[]) and ArrayResize'd before population where required
Buffer/array indexing accounts for series vs non-series direction (newest at index 0 only when set as series)

checks.name: indicator_architecture
checks.description: Handle-based indicator architecture (MQL5) and buffer model correctness
checklist: MQL5 indicator handles are created once (in OnInit), reused across ticks, and never created per-tick inside OnTick/OnCalculate
No deprecated per-tick iCustom/iMA handle creation in hot paths
SetIndexBuffer indices are contiguous and unchanged when new buffers are added; PlotIndexSetXxx matches the buffer it describes
Indicator buffer values are read via CopyBuffer with a validated return, not assumed populated

checks.name: oncalculate_correctness
checklist: OnCalculate returns rates_total (never 0 on a successful pass) so prev_calculated advances correctly
First-run (prev_calculated == 0) initialises buffers; subsequent runs only recompute from prev_calculated-1 forward
No full-history recomputation on every tick when an incremental update suffices

checks.name: indicator_handle_release
checklist: Every indicator handle obtained in OnInit is released with IndicatorRelease in OnDeinit
Handles are checked against INVALID_HANDLE before use

checks.name: chart_object_cleanup
checklist: All chart objects created by the program use a unique program prefix and are removed in OnDeinit via prefix-based ObjectsDeleteAll(0, "PREFIX_") or ObjectDelete
No object-name collisions with other EAs/indicators on the same chart
ChartRedraw() is called once after batch object updates, not per object

checks.name: timer_and_deinit
checks.description: Timers and other resources are released in OnDeinit
checklist: EventKillTimer() is called in OnDeinit when EventSetTimer was used
File handles from FileOpen are closed with FileClose
No Sleep() in indicators (only permitted in EAs and scripts)

phase_4_dll_cpp_interop.description: DLL / C-C++ interop correctness (conditional — only when native DLL sources are present)

checks.name: dll_source_present
checks.description: Detect native C/C++ DLL sources; skip the remaining interop checks if none are found
checks.note: This phase is conditional. If no C/C++ sources exist, the interop checklist below does not apply.

checks.name: dll_export_and_abi
checklist: Exported functions use __declspec(dllexport) (or a matching .def file) and the expected calling convention (__stdcall for MQL #import compatibility)
Parameter and return types map cleanly to MQL types (int/long/double/wchar_t* for strings); no STL containers or C++ objects across the boundary
The MQL #import declaration signatures exactly match the C/C++ exports (count, order, width of each parameter)
DLL bitness matches the terminal (64-bit DLL for MT5 / 64-bit terminal); 'Allow DLL imports' is documented as a runtime prerequisite
Pointers/buffers passed from MQL are sized correctly and never written past their bounds on the C side

checks.name: no_hardcoded_secrets
checks.description: No hard-coded credentials or account identifiers
checklist: No hard-coded account numbers, passwords, API keys, broker server names, or license tokens in source
Sensitive values are supplied via input parameters or external config, not literals
Account-binding (if any) uses AccountInfoInteger(ACCOUNT_LOGIN) checks, not embedded secrets

checks.name: input_and_file_safety
checks.description: Inputs and file access are validated
checklist: All input parameters are range/sanity validated in OnInit (periods >= 2, lots within broker min/max/step, non-negative timeouts)
FileOpen uses FILE_COMMON / sandbox-relative paths only; no absolute or user-controlled paths that escape the MQL sandbox
String parsing uses StringFind/StringSubstr rather than fixed offsets; broker symbol suffixes are configurable, never hard-coded

checks.name: trade_operation_guards
checklist: Every CTrade call (or native OrderSend) result is checked; retcode is inspected (TRADE_RETCODE_DONE) before assuming success
Position/lot sizing is computed against SYMBOL_VOLUME_MIN/MAX/STEP with no integer/overflow truncation
Stops and order prices respect SYMBOL_TRADE_STOPS_LEVEL and are normalised to _Digits/_Point
Duplicate-order and re-entry guards exist (hedging-safe scan before opening); magic numbers are named constants

checks.name: strict_compilation_clean
checklist: The compilation .log reports zero warnings (unused variables, implicit conversions, deprecated functions)
No implicit narrowing conversions in lot/price arithmetic

checks.name: property_headers
checklist: #property copyright, #property link, and #property version are present
#property description is present for EAs and indicators
Standard MQL file/function header comment blocks are used

checks.name: naming_and_conventions
checklist: input is used for user-facing parameters (not the deprecated extern) in MQL5
Files PascalCase, classes C-prefixed PascalCase, variables camelCase, object names project-prefixed; magic numbers are named constants
Calculation, rendering, and event-handling concerns are separated (.mqh calculators do not touch chart objects)
No Print() in hot paths (OnTick/OnCalculate); PrintFormat with __FUNCTION__ used for errors and init/deinit only

essential: MetaEditor (metaeditor64.exe for MT5 / metaeditor.exe for MT4)
advanced: MSVC / MinGW toolchain (for C/C++ DLL builds)
Note: MQL has no standard headless lint/coverage/static-analysis CLI; phases 2-6 are manual or checklist-driven by necessity

integration.ci_cd_note: MetaEditor compilation is a Windows GUI-tool dependency with no first-class headless CI runner; phase 1 requires a Windows host with MetaEditor installed, and phases 2-6 are manual/checklist reviews. CI integration is limited to driving metaeditor64.exe /compile on a self-hosted Windows runner.
